ORDER - STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JOE DRUMMOND AND JOYCE REITMAN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO THEIR RIGHT TO RE-FILE A MOTION FOR FEES ONCE A JUDGMENT IS ENTERED. THE GENERAL RULE IS THAT FEES SOUGHT PER A STATUTE MUST BE SOUGHT BY WAY OF A POST-JUDGMENT MOTION. (CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1033.5(A)(10); CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 3.1702(B)(1); BELL V. FARMERS INSURANCE EXCHANGE (2001) 87 CAL. APP. 4TH 805, 829-33). STATUTORY FEES CAN BE AWARDED PRIOR TO ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IF "SPECIFICALLY PROVIDED FOR BY STATUTE." (BELL, 87 CAL. APP. 4TH AT 830). CIVIL CODE 5235 DOES NOT SPECIFICALLY PROVIDE FOR AN AWARD OF FEES PRIOR TO ENTRY OF JUDGMENT. MOVING PARTY'S COSTS MEMORANDUM IS ALSO PREMATURE AND STRICKEN AND THUS THE HEARING ON FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR IN THE EVENT THAT THE TENTATIVE RULING ON THE FEES MOTION IS CONFIRMED. THE DECEMBER 30, 2015 HEARING IS OFF CALENDAR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489.

calendar.matters:
Motion To Tax Costs Requested In Plaintiffs Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALBERT G. STOLL, JR., ESQ. of Albert G. Stoll, Jr., ALC, Tel No. 415-576-1500 appearing for the Petitioners;
Atty. JOSEPH P. BREEN, ESQ. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. 415-986-5900 appearing for Respondent Francesca Association.

Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Tax Costs Requested In Plaintiffs Memorandum Of Costs is granted in part. The copying costs of $222.15 and the mediation fees of $1195.00 are taxed. Plaintiffs are the prevailing parties per CCP 1032(a)(4) because they obtained the primary non-monetary relief that they sought. The "reasonable costs and expenses" language in Civil Code 5235(a) does not entitle plaintiffs to any non-fees costs that are not allowable per CCP 1033.5. The copying costs and mediation fees are not allowable per CCP 1033.5. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALBERT G. STOLL, JR., ESQ. of Albert G. Stoll, Jr., ALC, Tel No. 415-576-1500 appearing for the Petitioners;
Atty. JOSEPH P. BREEN, ESQ. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. 415-986-5900 appearing for Respondent Francesca Association.

Matter not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS JOE DRUMMOND AND JOYCE REITMAN'S Motion For Attorney Fees is granted in part. Plaintiffs are awarded fees of $36,170, calculated as $15,000 for the services of their counsel prior to Albert Stoll and $21,700 for the fees they incurred for the work performed by Mr. Stoll. Plaintiffs are entitled to an award of fees per Civil Code 5235(a) because defendant unreasonably withheld access to the unredacted general ledger. Defendant's position that the plaintiffs were not entitled to inspect an unredacted general ledger was without basis in the law or equity. The privacy concerns underlying defendant's position could and should have been addressed through confidentiality restrictions, not by preventing plaintiffs from reading material portions of the ledger. Plaintiffs' decision not to file this case in the small claims division of this court was reasonable since they would have been required to proceed without counsel. Plaintiffs are entitled to fees notwithstanding their dismissal of two of their claims since they obtained the primary relief they sought. The fees plaintiffs incurred for the services of Mr. Stoll are reasonable, although there is no basis to award the multiplier requested by plaintiffs as to those fees. The fees sought for the services for the attorneys who preceded Mr. Stoll are excessive and far out of proportion to the relatively simple issues in this case. After a careful review of the materials submitted to support the request for fees for the services of the attorneys prior to Mr. Stoll, the court concludes that $15,000 (calculated at 50 hours of work at $300 per hour) is a reasonable amount for those services. The court exercises its discretion not to assess a civil penalty. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs Requested N Plainitffs Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Dec-10-2015 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Dec-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Albert Stoll of AGS telephone number 415-576-1500 for Petitioners Joe Drummond and Joyce Reitman.
Joseph Breen of Gordon & Rees telephone number 415-602-4848 for Defendant Francesca Association.

Mr. Stoll advised the Court that he is dismissing his remaining claims. The Court directed Mr. Stoll to file a dismissal, to prepare a form of judgment and submit it to Mr. Breen, and then re-file his motion for fees. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFFS JOE DRUMMOND and JOYCE REITMAN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is denied without prejudice to their right to re-file a motion for fees once a judgment is entered. The general rule is that fees sought per a statute must be sought by way of a post-judgment motion. (Code of Civil Procedure 1033.5(a)(10); California Rule of Court 3.1702(b)(1); Bell v. Farmers Insurance Exchange (2001) 87 Cal. App. 4th 805, 829-33). Statutory fees can be awarded prior to entry of judgment if "specifically provided for by statute." (Bell, 87 Cal. App. 4th at 830). Civil Code 5235 does not specifically provide for an award of fees prior to entry of judgment. Moving party's costs memorandum is also premature and stricken and thus the hearing on Francesca Association's motion to tax costs will be taken off calendar in the event that the tentative ruling on the fees motion is confirmed. The December 30, 2015 hearing is off calendar. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/HK)

Notice Of Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. ALBERT G. STOLL, ESQ. of Albert G. Stoll, Jr., A Law Corporation, Tel No. 415-576-1500 appearing for Petitioners;
Atty. JOSEPH P. BREEN, ESQ. of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel No. 415-986-5900 appearing for Respondent Francesca Association.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONERS JOE DRUMMOND AND JOYCE REITMAN'S Petition For Writ Of Mandamus is granted. Petitioners are entitled to review the Association's general ledger. Redaction of the names for the general ledger will significantly reduce petitioners' ability to review and understand the general ledger. Defendants' privacy concerns can be adequately accommodated by a protective order limiting petitioners' disclosure of the information in the general ledger and limiting petitioners use of the general ledger solely for matters pertaining to the Association. Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order. Parties to meet and confer as to issue of protective order. Issue of protective order set for further hearing on October 15, 2015 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion, to be heard by Judge Kahn. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas Purpostedly Served On Canyon Pacific Management Co., Inc. And Eugene Burger Management Corporation
Off Calendar Jan-16-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MATTHEW J. GLUCK, Tel No. (415) 226-1173 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. JOSEPH P. BREEN of Gordon & Rees, LLP, Tel No. (415) 986-5900 appearing for the Respondent.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: RESPONDENT FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Purportedly Served on Canyon Pacific Management Co., Inc. and Eugene Burger Management Corporation is denied except subpoenaed parties/defendant need not produce documents subject to claims of privilege pursuant to the Davis-Stirling Act, California Civil Code section 5215, or any other applicable claim of privilege. As to documents withheld based a claim of privilege, subpoenaed parties/defendant shall provide a privilege log identifying the nature of the document, writer, recipient, date of writing and facts supporting the basis for the privilege claim. Documents responsive to the subpoenas, a privilege log, and/or declarations of non-possession of documents shall be served within ten days of the date of this Order. Counsel stipulate to the court adding the following language to the Court's ruling/order: "The documents responsive to Plaintiff's Requests for Production do not include the Francesca Association's unredacted general ledger." Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied without prejudice. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/AJR)
